archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules
